_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in intrusion detection methodologies and techniques for detecting host and network-based intrusions for utilizing intrusion detection systems and signature development.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, including vulnerability identification, penetration testing coordination, and security architecture review.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-001 (SP, cosine: 0.438), TF-S-012 (TF, cosine: 0.411).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.584). Shared vocabulary: [skill, systems]. DCWF-only terms [based, detecting, detection, development, host, intrusion] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.875, R=0.879). Balanced P/R — the 21-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in intrusion detection methodologies and techniques for detecting host and" and "Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, inclu". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.448 < STS 0.584 (gap -0.136). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [based, detecting, detection] and [ai, architecture, assessments] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [skill, systems]. DCWF-only: [based, detecting, detection, development, host, intrusion]. WIDAI-only: [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
